site.heroLead: A business proves its identity and beneficial ownership once, holds the proof in a pod it controls, and reuses it at every bank that needs it.
site.heroParagraph: Northwind Logistics LLC opens business banking and a credit line at two banks without re-submitting formation documents, an EIN letter, or a cap table a second time.

domainNegations: Nothing here is a real bank account, credit decision, or verified business identity.
branding.description: demonstrate a reusable Solid-based business-identity and beneficial-ownership vault for US small-business KYB onboarding
branding.illustrativeTag: illustrative — not a real business record

persona.name: Jordan Blake
persona.descriptor: Fictional persona — Managing Member of Northwind Logistics LLC, a simulated freight/logistics small business.
persona.footnote: The four owners' stakes are pinned around the CDD Rule's 25% threshold: Jordan (42%) and Priya (28%) sit above it; Marcus (18%) and Dana (12%) sit below it. All figures are fictional and simulated.

anchors.label: AML/KYC penalties, H1 2025
anchors.detail: A 417% year-over-year increase in global regulatory penalties for AML/KYC failures.

anchors.label: of banks lose clients
anchors.detail: to slow business-onboarding, driven by document-heavy, repeated KYB checks.

anchors.label: KYB onboarding time
anchors.detail: A regional-bank case study on automating business-onboarding checks.

anchors.label: beneficial-owner verification threshold
anchors.detail: Each new bank must identify and verify every individual owning 25% or more of a legal-entity customer.

real: Every credential card runs the real fail-closed vc-kit verify chain against the pod-served documents — signature, issuer key binding, validity window, revocation status, SHACL shape.
Grant/revoke runs through the real @jeswr/solid-pod-guard boundary — DPoP-bound auth, pim:storage pod binding, and a real WAC rewrite on the pod. Revocation is immutable; repeats are refused.
The Tier A per-owner threshold proof and Tier B completeness proof are genuine sparq UltraHonk proofs, checked by the real fail-closed verifier.
simulated: Northwind Logistics LLC and its four beneficial owners are fictional personas issued by this repo's dev seeder — no real LEI, EIN, or cap table.
This scaffold has no borrower login UI yet, so a visitor without a real Solid session sees the rail's own honest 401.

roles.summary: Northwind Logistics LLC's own pod: identity, beneficial-ownership, and officer credentials, access grants, and the consent-receipt ledger.
Issues the LEI-anchored organisational-identity credential and the officer-authorization credential.

roles.modelledOn: unbranded business registry / FinCEN BO source
roles.summary: Issues the beneficial-ownership credential listing all four owners' basis-point stakes.
Runs the full KYB/CDD check by reading the org-identity and beneficial-ownership credentials directly from the granted vault access.

roles.role: Competing KYB relying party + credit line
roles.summary: Screens a credit-line application against a zero-knowledge beneficial-ownership proof before requesting full disclosed access.
Publishes the FIBO Business Entities ↔ GLEIF LEI ↔ FinCEN CDD binding table this vault's credentials are grounded in.

chapters.title: Fill the vault
chapters.anchor: vLEI OOR/ECR role concept (GLEIF); FIBO-BE LEIEntities/Executives modules
chapters.lead: Northwind Logistics LLC's vault is seeded with its organisational-identity, beneficial-ownership, and officer-authorization credentials, each one genuinely signed and verify-on-view.

steps.title: Seed the demo persona
steps.body: Write Jordan Blake's real signed KYB credentials into Northwind Logistics LLC's vault: an LEI-anchored organisational-identity credential, a beneficial-ownership credential listing all four owners, and an officer-authorization credential naming Jordan as CEO.

steps.title: View the credentials, verified
steps.body: Load the vault's credential list. Each card runs the full fail-closed verify chain — signature, issuer key binding, validity window, revocation status, and SHACL shape — every time the page loads.

underneath: Every credential is a genuine W3C VC 2.0 document, eddsa-rdfc-2022 signed, checked against its SHACL shape and Bitstring status list before it is ever trusted.
The LEI is an obviously-illustrative, checksum-valid placeholder — never a real GLEIF-issued identifier.

chapters.title: Onboard without re-submitting
chapters.anchor: FinCEN CDD Rule, 31 CFR 1010.230; iComply 5-day to under-24-hour case study
chapters.lead: A bank-modelled relying party is granted real read access to the disclosed org-identity and beneficial-ownership credentials, and opens an account without collecting formation documents again.

steps.title: Grant a bank read access
steps.body: Grant the business-banking relying party read access to the organisational-identity and beneficial-ownership credentials. The grant rebuilds a real WAC access rule on each resource and writes a consent receipt.

steps.title: Watch the receipt land
steps.body: The consent-receipt ledger records exactly who was granted what, and when, so the business can audit every disclosure it has ever made.

underneath: The grant rewrites the resource's own WAC ACL through typed access-control wrappers — never a hand-built or string-concatenated document.

chapters.title: Prove ownership without exposing the cap table
chapters.lead: Before disclosing anything, Northwind proves in the browser that no undisclosed owner holds 25% or more — a real zero-knowledge proof, not a spreadsheet.

steps.title: Prove the per-owner threshold
steps.body: Generate a live Tier A proof that a disclosed owner's basis-point stake is at least the 25% threshold, without revealing the exact percentage.

steps.title: Prove completeness
steps.body: Generate the Tier B completeness proof: the full, hidden owner array has been scanned and exactly the disclosed set is at or above the threshold — no undisclosed owner is hiding above it.

underneath: Both proofs are genuine sparq Noir/UltraHonk circuits, bound to an issuer-signed operand anchor so a bare proof can never be forged over fabricated data.
The completeness circuit is this project's own bespoke, unaudited research artifact — flagged honestly in the honesty panel, never presented as production-grade.

chapters.title: Reuse, not re-collection
chapters.anchor: Fenergo H1-2025 penalty growth; Fintech Global 70%-loss statistic
chapters.lead: The same org-identity and beneficial-ownership credentials, already granted to one bank, are the ones a second relying party reuses — no third copy of Northwind's records is ever made.

steps.title: Read the grant list
steps.body: The vault's own grant list already shows both banks' access scoped to the same underlying credentials — proof that reuse, not re-collection, is what actually happened.

steps.title: Count what was never re-collected
steps.body: No formation documents, EIN letter, or cap table were re-submitted for the second relying party. The vault is the one place Northwind's proof lives.

chapters.title: Nothing goes stale
chapters.lead: When Marcus Webb sells his stake to Jordan, the beneficial-ownership credential is re-issued through the existing grant, and both banks see the fresh ownership on their next read.

steps.title: Re-issue on ownership change
steps.body: Re-issue the beneficial-ownership credential with Jordan's stake now over 60%, revoke the stale version's status entry, and keep the same grant in place — no re-grant required.

steps.title: Confirm both banks see it
steps.body: Both relying parties read the same resource on their next visit and see the fresh ownership figures immediately, without either one re-running KYB from scratch.

chapters.title: Control, audit, and the regulator view
chapters.lead: Jordan reads the consent-receipt ledger, can revoke a bank's access at any time, and the vault shows that CDD was satisfied through scoped, receipted access rather than a filing.

steps.title: Revoke a bank's access
steps.body: Revoke a relying party's access to one credential. The WAC rule is removed immediately and a revocation receipt is written; revocation is immutable — the party is never silently re-granted.

steps.title: Read the full ledger
steps.body: Every grant and revocation, time-ordered, in one place: which banks and registrars were ever granted what, and when it changed.

compliance.title: Examiner view — the CDD Rule, satisfied without a filing
compliance.nonAffiliation: This lens is an unbranded illustration of a bank examiner's review process. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or a filing to any regulator.

checks.rule: A covered financial institution must identify and verify each individual owning 25% or more of a legal-entity customer, plus one control person, at account opening.
checks.observe: The relying party reads the disclosed organisational-identity and beneficial-ownership credentials through a scoped, receipted grant instead of collecting new paperwork.
